mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of months with rising 3-month FEDFUNDS trends, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and reduced downstream demand for durables, credit, and materials, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver average 60-day S&P 500 returns that are not below unconditional average returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth is positive while CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating, nominal demand strength is treated as inflationary rather than real growth, increasing tightening risk and lowering S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate rises versus the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 20-day returns than RSAFS strength without CPIAUCSL acceleration, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, leading investors to reduce expected earnings growth and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if mean triggered returns are not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and PAYEMS signals broad real activity expansion with production supported by hiring, causing upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase over 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: early-cycle or mid-cycle real activity expansion
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS expansion trigger does not outperform cases where only one of the two series rises, or if triggered 60-day excess returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL signals policy easing without an inflation shock, reducing discount-rate pressure and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below its 6-month average and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate is below its prior 3-month rate
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than all other observations, or if the estimated interaction effect is not positive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A negative monthly change in PAYEMS signals an abrupt deterioration in employment income and business confidence, leading to near-term equity risk reduction and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change crosses below zero
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS contraction triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive trends in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicate resilient aggregate demand and consumer spending, supporting near-term revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive GDPC1 growth and positive RSAFS 3-month growth fail to produce above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.
